title: Advantage, Disadvantage, and Heroic Inspiration
description: Modifier systems for D20 Tests that reflect circumstances and heroic moments

overview.advantage: Advantage reflects the positive circumstances surrounding a d20 roll

acquisition.common_sources: You usually acquire Advantage or Disadvantage through the use of special abilities and actions
acquisition.dm_discretion: The DM can also decide that circumstances grant Advantage or impose Disadvantage

rolling.procedure: When a roll has either Advantage or Disadvantage, roll a second d20 when you make the roll
rolling.advantage_resolution: Use the higher of the two rolls if you have Advantage
rolling.disadvantage_resolution: Use the lower roll if you have Disadvantage
rolling.example: If you have Disadvantage and roll an 18 and a 3, use the 3. If you instead have Advantage and roll those numbers, use the 18

stacking.advantage_stacking: If multiple situations affect a roll and they all grant Advantage on it, you still roll only two d20s
stacking.disadvantage_stacking: Similarly, if multiple situations impose Disadvantage on a roll, you roll only two d20s
stacking.cancellation: If circumstances cause a roll to have both Advantage and Disadvantage, the roll has neither of them, and you roll one d20
stacking.cancellation_rule: This is true even if multiple circumstances impose Disadvantage and only one grants Advantage or vice versa. In such a situation, you have neither Advantage nor Disadvantage

reroll_interactions.limitation: When you have Advantage or Disadvantage and something in the game lets you reroll or replace the d20, you can reroll or replace only one die, not both
reroll_interactions.example: If you have Heroic Inspiration and roll a 3 and an 18 on an ability check that has Advantage or Disadvantage, you could expend your Heroic Inspiration to reroll one of those dice, not both of them

heroic_inspiration.description: Sometimes the DM or a rule gives you Heroic Inspiration

usage.activation: If you have Heroic Inspiration, you can expend it to reroll any die immediately after rolling it
usage.requirement: You must use the new roll

limitations.quantity: You can never have more than one instance of Heroic Inspiration
limitations.overflow: If something gives you Heroic Inspiration and you already have it, you can give it to a player character in your group who lacks it

gaining_inspiration.dm_reasons: Your DM can give you Heroic Inspiration for a variety of reasons
gaining_inspiration.typical_awards: Typically, DMs award it when you do something particularly heroic, in character, or entertaining
gaining_inspiration.purpose: It's a reward for making the game more fun for everyone playing
gaining_inspiration.other_sources: Other rules might allow your character to gain Heroic Inspiration independent of the DM's decision
gaining_inspiration.example: Human characters start each day with Heroic Inspiration

mechanics.scope: Can be used on any die roll in the game
mechanics.mandatory_usage: Must use the new roll result
mechanics.single_die: When used with Advantage/Disadvantage, only reroll one of the two dice

advantage_with_reroll.scenario: Character has Advantage and Heroic Inspiration
advantage_with_reroll.rolls: Rolls 3 and 18 on an ability check
options: Use 18 (higher roll) without spending Heroic Inspiration
Spend Heroic Inspiration to reroll the 3
Spend Heroic Inspiration to reroll the 18
advantage_with_reroll.choice: Player chooses which die to reroll, if any

cancellation_scenario.situation: Multiple sources of Disadvantage and one source of Advantage
cancellation_scenario.result: Roll one d20 normally (no Advantage or Disadvantage)
cancellation_scenario.principle: Any amount of Advantage cancels any amount of Disadvantage

advantage_sources: Attacking a prone target with a melee attack
Having the high ground in certain situations
Using a Help action from an ally
